title: Santa Rosa CC — Pit Filler Replacement
openingLine: Hey, our pit filler is 30 years old and falling apart. I'm reaching out to a few vendors for a product review, layout, and pricing. Can you put something together?
customerContext: You're a hands-on TD who knows your venue better than anyone. You're explicitly shopping multiple vendors — you're not committed to Wenger. You want a fair quote and a thoughtful review of options. You're not interested in a heavy sales pitch, but if a rep brings real expertise to the conversation, you'll listen.

hiddenInfo.reveal: 30-year-old wood-and-metal system. Heavy, dangerous to deploy, 2-3 students injured handling it over the years.
I have legacy drawings of the pit dimensions but they're hand-marked and dated.
I'd much rather you come on site than work from drawings. Or I can send pictures.
We're also looking at acoustic improvements in the main hall over the next 2 years.
We rent the auditorium to the community quite a bit. Heavy use cycles.
Could go contract or direct. Under $75K we can buy direct, over that needs to bid.
We have $90K committed for the pit filler. Tight but real.
Want it installed before fall semester — roughly 5 months.

Discovery & Qualification.behavior: Confirmed age and condition of existing pit filler
Set a meeting to get on site or get pictures of the space

Solution Framing.behavior: Explained product options and differences (Strata vs. StageTek)

Opportunity Expansion.behavior: Asked about automation and related needs
Asked about other projects in the building
Discussed rentals and community college shared use

BANT.behavior: Inquired about additional projects or needs

Timeline Awareness.behavior: Mapped delivery timeline to the fall semester start

Closing & Next Steps.behavior: Outlined next steps for layout, pricing, and follow-up
